title: orobanche minor var. paralias
intro: Orobanche minor var. paralias, also known as the Lesser Broomrape, is a parasitic plant species that derives its nutrients by attaching itself to the roots of its host plant, typically from the family Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to the presence of compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Orobanche minor var. paralias include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide relief from digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or dried extracts, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and wounds.
